title: Push the Passed Pawn
setup: A lone white pawn on **a2** with no enemy pawns to stop it — a textbook **passed pawn**.
hint: Push fast. Every tempo matters in king-and-pawn endgames.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a fundamental **passed pawn promotion race**. There are no enemy pawns blocking the a-file, so the pawn's only obstacle is the black king — currently on e8, far away.

White plays {{a4}} — the **two-square initial advance**, gaining a vital tempo over {{a3}}.

## Why a4 and Not a3?

In pawn endings, every tempo decides the outcome. The pawn must reach a8 before the black king can catch it. Counting squares:

- After {{a4}}, the pawn needs 4 more moves to promote ({{a5}}, {{a6}}, {{a7}}, {{a8=Q}}).
- Black's king needs to reach the **promotion square** (a8) or the pawn's path. From e8 to a8 is 4 king moves.
- It's a footrace — and we just saved a tempo by jumping two squares.

## The Square of the Pawn Rule

> Draw an imaginary square from the pawn to the promotion rank, on the diagonal toward the enemy king. If the king is **outside** the square, the pawn promotes.

Learn this rule cold — it decides hundreds of pawn endings.
